title: Indefinite retention by default
description: New stores, logs, uploads, or analytics events keep personal data forever because no expiry was configured.
body: Failure mode: durable storage is added without retention owner, TTL, cleanup job, or deletion integration. Detection signals include no expiry fields, no cleanup tests, no owner metadata, and archives excluded from deletion logic. Remediation is explicit retention and cleanup evidence. Human gate is required for sensitive or regulated data without approved retention. Use when a proposed approach skips personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion.
